stem: A scatterplot shows data for twelve nonprofits along with a line of best fit relating annual donations (in millions of dollars) to the percent of donations spent on program work. For the nonprofit with the greatest percent of donations spent on programs, the actual donation amount is 8 million dollars and the value predicted by the line of best fit is 11 million dollars. Which of the following is closest to the difference of the actual amount and the predicted amount?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. Actual minus predicted = 8 − 11 = −3.

Choice B is the absolute value. Choices C and D give actual and predicted values, not the difference.
